---
title: "Trends"
subtitle: "How the sector has grown — and where it's headed"
---

::: {.key-finding}
<span class="kf-label">Key finding</span>
Nonprofit formation in the Inland Northwest is surging. After a pandemic dip
in 2020 (181 new IRS rulings), new formations climbed to **311 in 2024 and
396 in 2025 — the strongest two-year run on record** apart from a one-time
administrative spike in 2014, when the IRS introduced the streamlined Form
1023-EZ and processed a backlog of reinstatements. The 2010s produced more
new nonprofits (about 2,060 IRS rulings) than any prior decade, and the 2020s
are on pace to exceed them.
:::

{{< include _shared.qmd >}}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
formations = FileAttachment("data/formations.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
orgs = FileAttachment("data/orgs_active.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

## New nonprofit formations by year

Each point is the number of organizations receiving IRS tax-exempt rulings
that year, across all seven counties — including organizations that have since
dissolved. The red line is a five-year moving average. 2026 is a partial year
and is excluded. The 2014 spike (472 rulings) is largely administrative: the
IRS launched the short-form 1023-EZ application that July and worked through
a backlog of reinstatements following the 2011–2012 automatic revocations.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
formByYear = {
  const roll = d3.rollups(
    formations.filter(d => d.ruling_year >= 1960 && d.ruling_year <= 2025),
    v => d3.sum(v, d => d.n),
    d => d.ruling_year
  ).map(([year, n]) => ({year, n})).sort((a, b) => a.year - b.year);
  for (let i = 0; i < roll.length; i++) {
    const w = roll.slice(Math.max(0, i - 4), i + 1);
    roll[i].ma5 = d3.mean(w, d => d.n);
  }
  return roll;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 380,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 50,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year of IRS ruling", labelAnchor: "center", tickFormat: "d"},
  y: {label: "New organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(formByYear, {x: "year", y: "n", stroke: GU.s1, strokeWidth: 1.5, curve: "monotone-x"}),
    Plot.dot(formByYear, {x: "year", y: "n", fill: GU.s1, r: 2}),
    Plot.lineY(formByYear, {x: "year", y: "ma5", stroke: GU.red, strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "monotone-x"}),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"}),
    Plot.tip(formByYear, Plot.pointerX({
      x: "year",
      y: "n",
      title: d => `${d.year}\n${fnum(d.n)} new rulings\n5-yr average: ${d3.format(".0f")(d.ma5)}`
    }))
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`<span style="color:${GU.muted}; font-size:0.85rem;"><span style="color:${GU.s1}">━</span> annual rulings &nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color:${GU.red}">━</span> 5-year moving average. Hover for values.</span>`
```

::: {.chart-note}
Source: NCCS Business Master File, all vintages 1989–2026. Rulings before
~1989 appear only if the organization survived into the BMF era, so early
decades are undercounted; the recent surge is not an artifact of this.
:::

## Formations by decade

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
byDecade = d3.rollups(
  formations.filter(d => d.ruling_year >= 1960 && d.ruling_year <= 2025),
  v => d3.sum(v, d => d.n),
  d => Math.floor(d.ruling_year / 10) * 10
).map(([decade, n]) => ({decade: `${decade}s`, n, partial: decade === 2020})).sort((a, b) => d3.ascending(a.decade, b.decade))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 60,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: null},
  y: {label: "New organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(byDecade, {
      x: "decade",
      y: "n",
      fill: d => d.partial ? GU.red : GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetLeft: 6,
      insetRight: 6,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.decade}${d.partial ? " (through 2025 — six years)" : ""}\n${fnum(d.n)} new rulings`
    }),
    Plot.textY(byDecade, {
      x: "decade",
      y: "n",
      text: d => fnum(d.n) + (d.partial ? "*" : ""),
      dy: -8,
      fill: GU.ink,
      fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
\* The 2020s bar (red) covers only 2020–2025 — six years — and has already
reached two-thirds of the full 2010s total.
:::

## Where growth is happening

Spokane County still produces the most new nonprofits in absolute terms, but
Kootenai County's share of regional formations has risen steadily with its
population boom. The chart compares each county's formations per five-year
period.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
countyGroups = formations
  .filter(d => d.ruling_year >= 1990 && d.ruling_year <= 2024)
  .map(d => ({
    ...d,
    group: ["Spokane County", "Kootenai County"].includes(d.county) ? d.county : "Other five counties",
    period: `${Math.floor(d.ruling_year / 5) * 5}–${String(Math.floor(d.ruling_year / 5) * 5 + 4).slice(2)}`
  }))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
byPeriod = d3.rollups(
  countyGroups,
  v => d3.sum(v, d => d.n),
  d => d.period,
  d => d.group
).flatMap(([period, groups]) => groups.map(([group, n]) => ({period, group, n})))
  .sort((a, b) => d3.ascending(a.period, b.period))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 360,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 55,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: null},
  y: {label: "New organizations per 5-year period", grid: true},
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: ["Spokane County", "Kootenai County", "Other five counties"],
    range: [GU.s1, GU.s3, GU.s2]
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(byPeriod, {
      x: "period",
      y: "n",
      stroke: "group",
      strokeWidth: 2.5,
      curve: "monotone-x"
    }),
    Plot.dot(byPeriod, {
      x: "period",
      y: "n",
      fill: "group",
      r: 4,
      stroke: "#ffffff",
      strokeWidth: 1,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.group}\n${d.period}: ${fnum(d.n)} new rulings`
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

## What kinds of nonprofits are being formed now?

Comparing the category mix of organizations formed since 2015 against the
longer-standing sector shows where founders see unmet needs: recent cohorts
tilt toward human services and away from the fraternal, membership, and
religious organizations that dominate older cohorts.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
cohortMix = {
  const active = orgs.filter(d => d.ruling_year != null && d.subsector_name !== "Unclassified");
  const mk = (rows, cohort) => {
    const total = rows.length;
    return d3.rollups(rows, v => v.length, d => d.subsector_name)
      .map(([subsector, n]) => ({subsector, cohort, share: n / total * 100, n}));
  };
  return [
    ...mk(active.filter(d => d.ruling_year < 2015), "Formed before 2015"),
    ...mk(active.filter(d => d.ruling_year >= 2015), "Formed 2015 or later")
  ];
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 200,
  marginBottom: 45,
  height: 430,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Share of cohort (%)", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true},
  y: {label: null},
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: ["Formed before 2015", "Formed 2015 or later"],
    range: [GU.s2, GU.s1]
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.dot(cohortMix, {
      x: "share",
      y: "subsector",
      fill: "cohort",
      r: 6,
      stroke: "#ffffff",
      strokeWidth: 1.5,
      sort: {y: "-x"},
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.cohort}\n${d.subsector}: ${fpct(d.share)} (${fnum(d.n)} orgs)`
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
Active organizations with a classified NTEE category only. Source: NCCS BMF,
June 2026 vintage.
:::
